description: Scope-conditional truth models from PhysicsGuard's conditional_verdict module. Verdicts are context-dependent — a claim that is CLEAN at one organizational scope may be CORRUPTED at another. Also contains organizational thresholds and information-theoretic bounds.

organizational_models.name: Hierarchical Organization
organizational_models.description: Top-down authority structure with clear chain of command
true_if: Clear decision authority exists at each level
Information flows reliably up and down the chain
Enforcement cost < 30% of productive output (PG.MAX_ENFORCEMENT_RATIO)
Span of control is manageable (typically 5-9 direct reports)
Feedback loops exist from bottom to top
false_if: Enforcement cost exceeds 30% of productive output
Information is systematically blocked or distorted between levels
Hidden labor exceeds 50% of total work (TAF thermodynamic insolvency)
Attrition rate exceeds 3.0 (knowledge collapse)
Single point of failure controls >75% of decisions (PG.MAX_INTERDEPENDENCY_LOAD)
organizational_models.failure_mode: Bureaucratic entropy — overhead consumes more energy than productive work

organizational_models.name: Distributed Network Organization
true_if: Network resilience score ≥ 0.62 (PG.PHI_RESILIENCE)
No single node controls >75% of connections (PG.MAX_INTERDEPENDENCY_LOAD)
Consensus mechanism has bounded convergence time
Adaptive slack ≥ 15% of capacity (PG.MIN_ADAPTIVE_SLACK)
Information propagates without systematic loss
false_if: Network resilience < 0.62 (cannot self-heal)
Single node failure cascades to >50% of network
Consensus degenerates to hidden hierarchy
Free-rider fraction exceeds network slack capacity
Information asymmetry creates de facto authority without accountability
organizational_models.failure_mode: Network fragility — cascade failure geometry when interdependency exceeds threshold

organizational_models.name: Patriarchal Authority Structure
organizational_models.description: Authority concentrated in single figurehead with narrative-based legitimacy
true_if: Figurehead has genuine domain expertise validated by measurement
Authority is bounded and delegable
Succession mechanism exists
Challenge/correction pathways are open
Hidden labor is acknowledged and compensated
false_if: Authority legitimized by narrative rather than measurement (TAF.AXIOM.5 violation)
Challenge pathways are blocked or punished
Hidden labor exceeds 30% (knowledge holders are unrecognized)
Succession depends on loyalty rather than competence
Figurehead confuses personal boundary (TETRA) with institutional container (CUBE)
organizational_models.failure_mode: Narrative insolvency — the story of authority replaces the physics of authority

organizational_models.name: Nomadic Egalitarian Structure
organizational_models.description: Fluid authority based on situational competence, no fixed hierarchy
true_if: Authority shifts to most competent member for each situation
Adaptive slack is high (≥25% — above PG.MIN_ADAPTIVE_SLACK)
Group size stays within Dunbar-range coordination capacity
Flow (F02) between members is unobstructed
Conflict resolution preserves relational bonds
false_if: Group exceeds coordination capacity without subdividing
Situational competence is overridden by social dominance
Free-riders exploit egalitarian norms
External threats require faster decision-making than consensus allows
Hidden hierarchy emerges through informal influence without accountability
organizational_models.failure_mode: Coordination collapse — egalitarian norms cannot scale beyond relational scope without structural support

organizational_thresholds.description: Key constants that determine when organizational models transition from valid to invalid

thresholds.meaning: Minimum network resilience for self-correction. Below 0.62, distributed networks cannot heal from node failures.
thresholds.derivation: 1/φ ≈ 0.618 — the golden ratio's reciprocal as the minimum viable fraction of a self-similar network

thresholds.meaning: Maximum fraction of energy a system can spend on enforcement before becoming parasitic. Above 30%, the container costs more than its contents.
thresholds.taf_link: TAF.SOA cost categories: signal_blocking + comfort_maintenance + performance_theater + expertise_silencing

thresholds.meaning: Minimum available capacity for adaptation, error recovery, and mediation. Below 15%, the system has no room to respond to perturbation.
thresholds.taf_link: TAF.AXIOM.3 — prediction error minimization requires slack to update models

thresholds.units: fraction of total connections through single node
thresholds.meaning: Maximum fraction of system dependencies through any single point. Above 75%, single-point failure propagates system-wide.
thresholds.esp_link: cascade_map: when >75% of cascade edges pass through one layer, that layer becomes a single-point-of-failure

information_thresholds.description: Information-theoretic bounds that serve as hard physical limits — no system can violate these regardless of substrate

bounds.name: Landauer's Principle
bounds.meaning: Minimum energy cost of information destruction. Sets the floor for all computation.
bounds.rosetta_bridge: Dimensional bridge between FAMILY.F03 (Information) and FAMILY.F05 (Energy-Thermo)

bounds.name: No-Free-Lunch Theorem
bounds.formal: For all learners L: E[error(L)] averaged over all distributions = constant
bounds.meaning: No learning algorithm can outperform random guessing across all possible problems without data. Specialization requires data.
bounds.rosetta_bridge: Connects FAMILY.F06 (Cognition) to FAMILY.F14 (Measurement) — you cannot think without first measuring

bounds.name: Data Processing Inequality
bounds.formal: I(X;Z) ≤ I(X;Y) for Markov chain X→Y→Z
bounds.meaning: Processing cannot create information. Each transformation can only preserve or lose information.
bounds.rosetta_bridge: Validates FAMILY.F03 (Information) merges — no merge can claim to produce more information than its inputs contain

bounds.name: Shannon Channel Capacity
bounds.meaning: Maximum rate of error-free communication over a noisy channel. Sets hard upper bound on achievable accuracy.
bounds.rosetta_bridge: Connects FAMILY.F01 (Resonance) to FAMILY.F03 (Information) — signal quality limits information transfer

conditional_verdict_logic.principle: A claim that is CLEAN at one scope may be CORRUPTED at another. The verdict depends on which organizational model applies.

example.claim: Strong central authority produces optimal outcomes
example.at_individual_scope: CLEAN — individual decision-making benefits from clear authority
example.at_institutional_scope: SUSPECT — depends on enforcement ratio and hidden labor

rosetta_integration.mechanism: Claims are validated at each fractal scope level independently. Final verdict is the most restrictive across all applicable scopes.
